sanskrit: उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन। नरके नियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम॥
transliteration: utsanna-kula-dharmāṇāṃ manuṣyāṇāṃ janārdana / narake niyataṃ vāso bhavatīty anuśuśruma
literal_meaning: O Janārdana, we have heard that men whose family duties are destroyed dwell forever in hell.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna expresses terror that annihilating his lineage will doom both himself and future generations to existential ruin, projecting cosmic punishment onto his moral paralysis. The verse reveals how inherited belief systems can weaponize shame and fear of eternal damnation to block decisive action, turning cultural narratives into internalized psychological prisons.
psychological_state: A person frozen by catastrophic visions of eternal punishment for violating sacred family codes.
trigger_scenario: A first-generation college student contemplating a career their immigrant parents deem dishonorable, haunted by nightmares of betraying ancestors and being excommunicated.
anchor_text: Fear of hell is a story; choose love over inherited shame.
